name: Dummy Deck
description: Sandbox for testing new technical phrases before migration.

cards.sideA: Si hubiéramos hecho esto medio año atrás, sería bastante diferente.

sideB.text: If we had done this half a year ago, it would be quite different.
If we'd done this half a year ago, things would be quite different.
If we had done this six months ago, it would be a lot different.

cards.note:
**[er:98]**

**[er:151]**

This sentence uses a Mixed Conditional. It combines a past hypothetical condition (Past Perfect: had done) with a present imaginary result (would be).

~If we had done this a year and a half ago~ is grammatically correct but changes the time frame to 'un año y medio', whereas the card specifies 'medio año' [half a year].

~If we had done this one and a half year ago~ is incorrect because plural quantities require plural nouns like [years], and it also alters the original time frame.

cards.sideA: ¿Mac o Windows? Así sé qué shell asumir.

sideB.text: Mac or Windows? That way I know which shell to assume.
Mac or Windows? That way I'll know what shell to assume.
Mac or Windows? So I know which shell to assume.
Mac or Windows? That way I know what type of shell to assume.

cards.sideA: Un script no se compila, se ejecuta línea por línea.

sideB.text: A script doesn't get compiled, it runs line by line.
A script isn't compiled; it's executed line by line.
A script doesn't compile, it runs line by line.
A script doesn't get compiled, it gets executed line by line.
A script doesn't get compiled, it executes line by line.

cards.sideA: Es un archivo solo, está dentro de su carpeta padre.

sideB.text: It is a single file, it is inside its parent folder.
It's a single file, it's inside its parent folder.
It is just one file, located inside its parent folder.
It is a single file, it is inside the parent folder.

cards.note: ~It is a single file, it is inside its father folder.~ In computing, the term for the enclosing directory is [parent folder], not 'father folder'.

cards.sideA: Para llegar allá, debes navegar y usar sintaxis de doble punto.

sideB.text: To get there, you must navigate and use double dot syntax.
To get there, you have to navigate using double dot syntax.
In order to get there, you need to navigate and use double dot syntax.

cards.note: ~To get there, you must navigate and use double point syntax.~ In programming and file systems, the '..' symbol is referred to as a [double dot] or [dot-dot], not 'double point'.

cards.sideA: Sorry, me cuesta pronunciar esa palabra.

sideB.text: Sorry, I find it hard to pronounce that word.
Sorry, it's hard for me to pronounce that word.
Sorry, I struggle to pronounce that word.

cards.note: ~Sorry, it's hard to me to pronounce that word.~ When expressing difficulty for someone, use the preposition [for] (e.g., 'hard for me'), not 'to'.

cards.sideA: Después del comando, finaliza con una coma.

sideB.text: After the command, end with a comma.
Finish with a comma after the command.
End with a comma after the command.

cards.note: ~After the command, finalize with a comma.~ is not natural here. The verb [finalize] usually means to complete a formal agreement, plan, or transaction. To describe adding a character to the end of a string or sequence, use [end] or [finish].

cards.sideA: Git es un sistema de control de versiones de código abierto.

sideB.text: Git is an open-source version control system.
Git is an open source version control system.
Git is a version control system that is open-source.

cards.sideA: Tenemos un formulario en blanco que rellenar, cada descripción del formulario debe ser rellenada en el espacio en blanco.

sideB.text: We have a blank form to fill out, each form description must be filled in the blank space.
We have a blank form to fill in; each description on the form needs to be filled in the blank space.
There's a blank form we need to fill out, every form description must be completed in the blank space.

cards.note: ~We have a white form to fill out, each form description must be filled in the white space.~ is not valid because 'white' refers to the color. For a form or space without written text, you must use [blank].

cards.sideA: La dirección de envío es la ubicación física donde se entrega el paquete.

sideB.text: Shipping Address is the physical location where the package is delivered.
The shipping address is the physical location where the package is delivered.
The shipping address is the physical location where the package gets delivered.
Delivery Address is the physical location where the package is delivered.

cards.sideA: La dirección de facturación es la dirección oficial vinculada a la tarjeta de crédito.

sideB.text: Billing Address is the official address linked to the credit card.
The billing address is the official address linked to the credit card.
The billing address is the official address associated with the credit card.
Billing Address is the official address related to the credit card.

cards.note: ~Invoice Address is the official address linked to the credit card.~ is not valid because [Billing address] is the standard term used for credit cards, whereas an invoice address is typically used for B2B billing.

cards.sideA: Lamentablemente, se dañó el código y no podemos probarlo ahora mismo.

sideB.text: Unfortunately, the code is broken and we can't test it right now.
Sadly, the code got damaged and we cannot test it at this moment.
Unfortunately, the code broke and we can't test it right now.

cards.sideA: HTML usa tags para su contenido. HTML usa brackets para delimitar los tags.

sideB.text: HTML uses tags for its content. HTML uses brackets to delimit tags.
HTML uses tags for its content. HTML uses brackets to define the tags.
HTML utilizes tags for its content. HTML uses brackets to delimit the tags.

cards.sideA: Pero solo para estar seguros, mejor hacerlo.

sideB.text: But just to be sure, it's better to do it.
But just to be on the safe side, we better do it.
But just to be safe, it's better to do it.

cards.sideA: Hay dependencias lógicas. O sea, la aplicación depende de ciertos procedimientos.

sideB.text: There are logical dependencies. I mean, the app relies on specific procedures.
There are logical dependencies. That is, the application depends on certain procedures.
There are logical dependencies. Meaning, the app relies on certain procedures.

cards.sideA: Hay una limitación física, no puedes correr la app de iOS en Android.

sideB.text: There's a technical limitation. You can't run the iOS app on Android.
There is a physical limitation, you cannot run the iOS app on Android.
There's a hardware limitation, you can't run the iOS app on Android.

cards.sideA: No puedes insertar esas filas aún, no hay tabla creada, es una limitación física.

sideB.text: You can't insert those rows yet because the table hasn't been created. It's a hard blocker.
You cannot insert those rows yet, there is no table created, it is a physical limitation.
You can't insert those rows just yet; no table has been created, it's a hard blocker.

cards.sideA: Sorry, no vi el mensaje.

sideB.text: Sorry, I missed your message.
Sorry, I didn't see your message.
Sorry, I just saw this.
Sorry, I missed the message.

cards.note: ~Sorry, I didn't watch your message.~ is not valid because the verb watch is used for moving images like videos or screens; for text, you must use [see] or [read].

cards.sideA: Sabemos que esta función es suficiente, pero debemos mejorarla.

sideB.text: We know this feature is good enough, but we need to improve it.
We know this feature is sufficient, but we must improve it.
We know this feature is adequate, but we need to make it better.

cards.sideA: Y un código que es posible distinguir entre qué sistema operativo aplica.

sideB.text: And code that can distinguish which operating system applies.
And a code that makes it possible to distinguish which OS it applies to.
And code that can differentiate which operating system it applies to.

cards.note: ~And a code that distinguishes which operating system applies.~ is not valid because programming [code] is uncountable and does not take 'a', and the verb apply requires the preposition [to] at the end.

cards.sideA: Con el tiempo, te acostumbrarás.

sideB.text: You'll get used to it over time.
With time, you will get used to it.
You'll grow accustomed to it over time.
Over time, you'll get used to it.

cards.note:
**[er:227]**

~No the time, you'll get used to it.~ is not valid because 'no the time' is not a recognized English phrase for 'con el tiempo'. Use [Over time] or [With time] instead.

cards.sideA: Estoy acostumbrado a eso.

sideB.text: I'm used to that.
I am accustomed to that.
I've gotten used to that.

cards.sideA: Tú tendrías que hacernos saber sobre ese cambio.

sideB.text: You would have to let us know about that change.
You should let us know about that change.
You'd need to inform us about that change.

cards.sideA: Llamó mi atención, por eso le avisé a él.

sideB.text: It caught my attention, that's why I let him know.
It drew my attention, so I warned him.
It caught my eye, which is why I told him.
It caught my attention, that's why I told him.

cards.note: ~It called my attention, that's why I let him know.~ is not valid because 'called my attention' is a direct translation from Spanish; the natural English idiom is [caught my attention] or [drew my attention].

cards.sideA: Es un cambio trivial, por eso no lo apliqué.

sideB.text: It's a trivial change, that's why I didn't apply it.
It is a trivial change, so I didn't apply it.
It's a minor change, which is why I didn't implement it.

cards.sideA: Es trivial, podemos hacerlo después.

sideB.text: It's trivial, we can do it later.
It's trivial, we can get to it later.
It is trivial, we can do it afterwards.

cards.sideA: Cablea el botón al evento.

sideB.text: Wire the button to the event.
Link the button to the event.
Connect the button to the event.

cards.note: ~It wires the button to the event.~ is not valid because adding 'It' makes the sentence a description, but the original text is an instruction or command that requires the imperative form [Wire].

cards.sideA: Enlaza el botón con el evento.

sideB.text: Wire the button to the event.
Bind the button to the event.
Link the button to the event.

cards.note: ~Wire the button with the event.~ is not valid because the verb [wire] in this context pairs with the preposition [to], not 'with'.

cards.sideA: Engancha este menú a la nueva función.

sideB.text: Wire this menu to the new feature.
Hook this menu to the new feature.
Link this menu to the new function.

cards.sideA: Hagamos que funcione.

sideB.text: Let's wire this up.
Let's get it working.
Let's make it work.
Let's make this work.

cards.note: ~Let's make it to work.~ is not valid because the causative verb [make] must be followed directly by the base verb without 'to'.

cards.sideA: Dijeron que sería clonado el ambiente, pero aún no lo ha sido.

sideB.text: They said the environment would be cloned, but it hasn't been yet.
They said the environment was going to be cloned, but it hasn't been done yet.
They mentioned the environment would be cloned, but it still hasn't been.

cards.note:
**[er:162]**

~They said the environment would be cloned, but it didn't yet.~ is not valid because the first clause uses the passive voice 'be cloned', so the second clause requires the matching auxiliary [hasn't been] or [wasn't], not 'didn't'.

cards.sideA: Cuando sea clonado el ambiente, podríamos replicar el problema.

sideB.text: Once the environment is cloned, we could replicate the issue.
When the environment gets cloned, we could reproduce the problem.
When the environment is cloned, we'll be able to replicate the issue.

cards.note: ~Once the ambient is cloned, we could replicate the issue.~ is not valid because 'ambient' refers to lighting or sound; the correct term for a software setup is [environment].

cards.sideA: Estamos conscientes de ese error, pero no hemos podido replicarlo aún.

sideB.text: We are aware of that bug, but we haven't been able to replicate it yet.
We're aware of that error, but we haven't managed to reproduce it yet.
We know about that bug, but we couldn't replicate it yet.
We are aware of that bug, but we weren't able to replicate it yet.

cards.note: ~We are aware about that bug, but we haven't been able to replicate it yet.~ is not valid because the adjective 'aware' must always be followed by the preposition [of], not 'about'.

cards.sideA: Colapsa el panel y luego expándelo.

sideB.text: Collapse the panel and then expand it.
Collapse the panel and expand it afterwards.
First collapse the panel, then expand it.

cards.sideA: Puedes colapsar todas las secciones o expandirlas de nuevo.

sideB.text: You can collapse all the sections or expand them again.
You can collapse all sections or expand them once more.
You're able to collapse all the sections or expand them again.

cards.sideA: Si colapsas el menú, puedes expandirlo cuando lo necesites.

sideB.text: If you collapse the menu, you can expand it when you need it.
If you collapse the menu, you can always expand it when needed.
If you collapse the menu, you can expand it whenever you need to.
